description: GTM Optimization Tier 2 — Pipeline infrastructure: Attio ID in Smartlead, reply webhook pipeline, campaign_leads table, Smartlead MCP

userStories.title: Create reply_events Supabase table
userStories.description: As the GTM system, I need a durable event store for Smartlead reply webhooks so no reply is lost even if downstream processing fails.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Supabase migration file supabase/migrations/20260413_reply_events.sql

Table reply_events with columns: id (uuid PK default gen_random_uuid()), smartlead_message_id (text UNIQUE NOT NULL), campaign_id (text NOT NULL), lead_email (text NOT NULL), attio_record_id (text), reply_body (text), classification (text), confidence (numeric), attio_synced (boolean DEFAULT false), slack_notified (boolean DEFAULT false), created_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now()), processed_at (timestamptz)
Partial index: CREATE INDEX idx_reply_events_unsynced ON reply_events (attio_synced) WHERE attio_synced = false
Migration applies successfully via Supabase MCP apply_migration tool
Verify table exists: SELECT count(*) FROM reply_events returns 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Schema from gtm/GTM-HARDENED-IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md Task 2.2. The UNIQUE on smartlead_message_id enables idempotent upsert — critical because Smartlead only retries 3 times.

userStories.title: Create campaign_leads Supabase table
userStories.description: As the agent swarm orchestrator, I need a shared blackboard table with atomic claim locking so parallel agents cannot claim the same lead.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Supabase migration file supabase/migrations/20260413_campaign_leads.sql

Table campaign_leads with columns: id (uuid PK), campaign_id (text NOT NULL), lead_email (text NOT NULL), attio_record_id (text), channel (text NOT NULL CHECK channel IN 'demand','supply'), status (text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'), claimed_by (text), claimed_at (timestamptz), research_output (jsonb), draft_output (jsonb), qa_score (numeric), qa_failures (jsonb DEFAULT '[]'), smartlead_campaign_id (text), smartlead_lead_id (text), error_log (jsonb DEFAULT '[]'), retry_count (int DEFAULT 0), created_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now()), updated_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now())
UNIQUE constraint on (campaign_id, lead_email)
Comment in migration documenting the atomic claim pattern: SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
Migration applies successfully
Verify: INSERT a test row with channel='demand', then INSERT same campaign_id+lead_email again — should fail with unique violation
Verify: INSERT a row with channel='invalid' — should fail with CHECK constraint
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Schema from gtm/GTM-HARDENED-IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md Task 2.3. The channel column enforces supply/demand separation (Rule #7). Status state machine: pending → dedup_checked → researching → research_done → writing → draft_done → qa → human_approved → loaded → failed.

userStories.title: Create handle-smartlead-reply Supabase Edge Function
userStories.description: As the GTM system, I need a webhook endpoint that receives Smartlead EMAIL_REPLIED events, persists them durably, classifies replies, and syncs to Attio.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge Function exists at supabase/functions/handle-smartlead-reply/index.ts
Accepts POST with Smartlead webhook payload (lead_email, reply body, campaign_id, message_id)
Step 1: Upsert into reply_events table keyed on smartlead_message_id (idempotent — same message_id = no duplicate)
Step 2: Classify reply into one of 7 categories (interested, question, objection, ooo, unsubscribe, referral, not_interested) using Claude Haiku via Anthropic SDK
Step 3: Update reply_events row with classification and confidence score
Step 4: If attio_record_id is available, create Attio note via REST API with format: '[CATEGORY] reply: [first 200 chars]'
Step 5: For interested or question categories, send Slack webhook notification with lead_email, company, and classification
Step 6: Mark attio_synced=true and slack_notified=true after successful delivery
If Attio API fails, reply_events row is preserved with attio_synced=false for later retry
Classification prompt must NEVER generate response content — classify and route only (Rule #1)
Returns 200 on success, 500 on unrecoverable error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Smartlead retries failed webhooks only 3 times then drops permanently. The idempotent upsert on smartlead_message_id is the safety net. Attio REST API docs: PATCH /v2/objects/people/records/{id}/notes. Slack webhook URL should come from environment variable SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL. Anthropic API key from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var.

userStories.title: Update claru-outbound skill to include attio_record_id in Smartlead loads
userStories.description: As the outbound orchestrator, I need every lead loaded to Smartlead to carry its Attio record ID so downstream webhook events can be matched back to CRM records without fuzzy matching.
acceptanceCriteria: Read ~/.claude/skills/claru-outbound/SKILL.md and identify the loader/campaign-loading section
Add a documented requirement: before loading any lead to Smartlead, look up or create the Attio record and store its ID
Add attio_record_id to the custom_fields specification in the Smartlead lead payload example
Add a section documenting the dedup check order: Attio search first, if exists use that ID, if not create new record then use new ID
The attio_record_id field must be populated for every lead — loading without it should be flagged as a violation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This modifies the skill documentation, not application code. The skill at ~/.claude/skills/claru-outbound/SKILL.md is the instruction set for the /claru-outbound slash command. Existing campaigns cannot be retrofitted (Smartlead limitation) — this applies to all NEW campaigns only.

userStories.title: Install Smartlead MCP server in project config
userStories.description: As an agent operating GTM workflows, I need Smartlead available as an MCP tool so I can list campaigns, get stats, and manage leads without CLI friction.
acceptanceCriteria: Read current .mcp.json to understand existing MCP server configuration
Add smartlead MCP server entry with command: npx, args: [@smartlead/mcp], env: SMARTLEAD_API_KEY from environment
The API key must NOT be hardcoded — must reference an environment variable
.mcp.json is gitignored (verify with: grep '.mcp.json' .gitignore)
Document the new MCP server in gtm/tools/mcps.md with: server name, what it does, key tools available, any gotchas (rate limit: 60 req/60s, sequences immutable on live campaigns)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The Smartlead MCP server exposes 116 tools. Key ones: campaign CRUD, lead management, webhook config, analytics. Rate limit is 60 requests per 60 seconds. Important constraint from Rule #7: sequence modification tools must ONLY be used on DRAFTED campaigns, never live ones.

userStories.title: Validation — test reply pipeline end-to-end
userStories.description: As the GTM system owner, I need to verify the entire reply handling pipeline works: webhook received → event persisted → classified → Attio noted → Slack notified.
acceptanceCriteria: reply_events table exists and accepts inserts
campaign_leads table exists with proper constraints (test: unique violation on duplicate, CHECK violation on bad channel)
Edge Function handle-smartlead-reply is deployed (verify via Supabase dashboard or CLI)
Simulate a webhook POST to the Edge Function with a test payload — verify reply_events row created
Verify classification field is populated with one of the 7 valid categories
If Slack webhook is configured, verify notification arrives for interested category
Smartlead MCP server entry exists in .mcp.json
claru-outbound SKILL.md documents attio_record_id requirement
gtm/tools/mcps.md documents the Smartlead MCP server
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the QA story. Run all verification steps. The Edge Function can be tested locally with: supabase functions serve handle-smartlead-reply --env-file .env.local, then curl POST to localhost.
